evidence: D-Feeders project at Locust Grove. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Feeders project at Elwood. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Feeders project at Woodmere. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Substation project at Bridgehampton. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Hither Hills: 23kV→33kV substation conversion. Source: LIPA environmental assessment (April 2024). Both voltages are well below LIPA's BES threshold (138kV). LIPA Transmission Planning Criteria Section 5: 'sub-transmission system consists of 23, 34.5 kV and 69 kV.'
D-Feeders project at Arverne. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Belmont: 33kV→69kV substation conversion with two new underground circuits to Lake Success and Whiteside substations. Source: psegliny.com/reliability/belmont (Jan 2025 construction start). 69kV = sub-transmission per LIPA Transmission Planning Criteria Section 5.
D-Feeders project at Syosset. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Feeders project at Tech Park. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Miller Place: distribution feeders, distribution poles, and substation expansion. PSEG LI's project page (psegliny.com/reliability/millerplace) describes 'installing new underground and overhead distribution feeders and expanding the existing substation on Route 25A.' All described work is distribution-level (new wood/steel poles, 13kV feeders). The T-Substation classification in the MCOS refers to the substation's transmission-side equipment, which on LIPA's system is 69kV or below.

Bridgehampton: 69kV substation with 69/13kV transformer banks. Source: PSEG LI Bridgehampton project page and Southampton Town archive (2007-2008 Article VII filing for Southampton-Bridgehampton 69kV transmission line; Bridgehampton substation expansion with 'new 69/13kV 33MVA transformer bank and switchgear'). Bridgehampton-Buell 69kV underground cable also confirms 69kV bus.
D-Feeders project at Ocean Beach. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

North Bellmore (ISD 2026): no direct voltage evidence found. No NY PSL Article VII filing exists for this project (required for ≥100kV transmission lines).  Classified as sub_tx by systemic inference: all 12 T-Substation projects with confirmed voltages are 69kV or below; LIPA's system has no intermediate voltages between 69kV (sub-TX) and 138kV (BES); and 138kV projects on LIPA's system generate extensive public record that is absent here.

Southampton: 69kV substation on the South Fork 69kV sub-transmission network. The Southampton substation is the origin point for: (a) Southampton-Bridgehampton 69kV line (Article VII, 2007), (b) Southampton-Deerfield 69kV-operated line (Article VII Case 24-T-0113, construction Jan 2026). All connected substations operate at 69kV.
D-Feeders project at Port Jefferson. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Feeders project at Locust Grove. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Moriches (phase 1, ISD 2027): 69kV substation confirmed by NYISO Gold Book 2025 Table VII interconnection queue entry C24-061: 'Hanwha Q CELLS USA Corp. Moriches 69kV K 12-2027 75.3MW Energy Storage.'  The interconnection voltage of 69kV confirms the substation operates at 69kV = sub-transmission.

Deerfield (phase 1, ISD 2027): Southampton-to-Deerfield underground transmission cable, 4.5 miles.  RATED at 138kV but OPERATED initially at 69kV.  Source: southampton2deerfield.com and DPS Case 24-T-0113 (Article VII certificate).  Project serves South Fork load growth. Classified as sub_tx because the operating voltage during the MCOS study period (FY2025-2032) is 69kV = sub-transmission per LIPA Transmission Planning Criteria Section 5.  When eventually energized at 138kV, this will become BES.
D-Feeders project at East Hampton Village. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
D-Feeders project at Arverne. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

New South Road (ISD 2028): no direct voltage evidence found. No public project page, no Article VII filing. Classified as sub_tx by systemic inference (see SOS 1540 reasoning). Capacity of 33 MVA is consistent with a 69kV sub-TX substation (comparable to Miller Place, North Bellmore at 33 MVA each).

Lindbergh (Uniondale): 69kV underground transmission cables and 13kV distribution feeders. Source: psegliny.com/reliability/lindbergh. Project includes 'Installation of two new underground 69kV transmission cables' and 'Installation of new underground 13kV distribution feeders.' Driven by Nassau Coliseum redevelopment area load growth.
D-Substation project at Wildwood. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Quogue Substation: 69kV confirmed by NYISO Gold Book 2025 Table VII entry C24-158: 'KCE NY 27, LLC Quogue - Tiana 69kV K 01-2028 51.5MW Energy Storage.'  Quogue-Tiana 69kV interconnection confirms the substation's bus voltage.

Peconic: 69kV substation confirmed by NYISO Gold Book 2025 Table VII entry C24-023: 'KCE NY 26, LLC Jamesport - Peconic 69kV K 01-2028 62.0MW Energy Storage.'  The Jamesport-Peconic 69kV interconnection confirms the substation operates at 69kV.
D-Feeders project at Arverne. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.

Arverne (ISD 2030): no direct voltage confirmed, but the Rockaway peninsula sub-transmission system connects Far Rockaway, Arverne, and Rockaway Beach substations (per MFS Engineers project reference for '2H Far Rockaway Substation - 2AR Arverne Substation').  PSEG LI's completed Arverne Reliability Project (2018-2019) upgraded underground cable and distribution infrastructure in the area.  No Article VII filing.  Classified as sub_tx by systemic inference.
D-Feeders project at Belmont. Distribution assets (D-Substation, D-Feeders) are unambiguously below sub-transmission voltage and included in BAT.
Moriches (phase 2, ISD 2031): same substation as SOS 2583. See SOS 2583 evidence (NYISO queue C24-061 confirms 69kV).
Deerfield (phase 2, ISD 2032): same Southampton-to-Deerfield corridor as SOS 1822.  Continued sub-transmission function at 69kV operating voltage.  See SOS 1822 evidence for full details.
